title: I Built an AI App. Eight Months Later, It Became a Skill
excerpt: When I first wrote about NutriAgent in November 2025, it was a full application. It had a Python...
content_html:
When I first wrote about NutriAgent in November 2025, it was a full application. It had a Python backend, a web interface, a Telegram bot, user accounts, Google OAuth, Supabase, and a Google Sheets integration.

Recently, I reproduced its core workflow as a skill for my personal AI agent. It took around 15 minutes and two prompts. I didn't build another backend, deploy a service, or implement OAuth again. I explained how I wanted the workflow to behave, tested it, and watched a new row appear in my nutrition spreadsheet.

The original application wasn't a mistake. It was how I could deliver that experience with the tools available at the time.

Eight months later, the starting point had changed.

Eight Months Ago, This Was an App

I built NutriAgent because I wanted to track calories and protein without trapping my data inside a nutrition app. I wanted the raw records in a spreadsheet I controlled, where I could create my own reports and eventually connect nutrition with my training data.

The first version was a personal n8n workflow. It worked for me, but when a friend wanted to try it, I realized that everything was tied to my accounts. To make it reusable, I rebuilt it in Python and added the parts a real multi-user product needed: authentication, storage, a web interface, Telegram, Google OAuth, conversation history, and account linking.

I've already told that story in I Ditched MyFitnessPal and Built an AI Agent to Track My Food, and later wrote about what broke after I used it every day for a month.

This article starts after that version.

My Gaming PC Became an Agent Box

I had a modest gaming PC at home with 16 GB of RAM and a 1 TB drive. Using it through Windows, WSL, and remote desktop from my Mac felt awkward, so I installed Linux and turned it into a remote box for running agents. I'll write about that setup separately.

I moved Hermes there from a VPS. Hermes is the personal agent I now run on that machine. It can load reusable skills and use tools connected to my Google Workspace. The PC runs the agent environment, not the frontier model itself.

Once Hermes was running, I wanted to test a workflow I already understood well. NutriAgent was the obvious candidate.

Two Prompts, Fifteen Minutes

Instead of opening the old repositories, I described the outcome I wanted. Hermes should understand a meal, estimate its nutritional values, follow the structure of my existing log, and append the result to Google Sheets.

The first prompt created the initial skill. I used a second prompt to clarify the remaining details and adjust how it should write the data.

Then I tried it with an actual meal.

The result landed in the same kind of spreadsheet I originally built NutriAgent to produce. There wasn't a new service running behind it. Hermes already knew how to interpret natural language and use tools. Google Sheets already held the data. The skill supplied the part that was specific to me: the procedure.

That's the part that surprised me. I didn't find a cleverer way to rebuild NutriAgent. I didn't rebuild it at all.

What I No Longer Had to Build

The skill isn't equivalent to the full NutriAgent product. I can't give someone a signup link. It doesn't have a generic Telegram bot, its own Google OAuth flow, or a dedicated interface. It only needs to work with my agent, my Google account, and my spreadsheet.

For personal use, that was enough to preserve the behavior I cared about:

  • Understand what I ate.
  • Estimate calories and macros.
  • Follow the schema of my nutrition log.
  • Save the result somewhere I control.

Previously, I had to own both that behavior and the application around it. Now Hermes provides much of the environment, and the skill only describes what is different about this workflow.

This also changed the marginal cost. I moved Hermes off the VPS and configured this experiment to use model access I already had through my Codex subscription instead of maintaining a separate per-call API bill for NutriAgent. The subscription and the electricity aren't free, so this isn't a zero-cost workflow. But it removed a separate bill for each NutriAgent interaction.

The skill also changed how I add features. I had wanted predefined meals for food I had already analyzed on previous days. In NutriAgent, that would've meant changing the application, updating the prompt, testing the new behavior, and deploying another version.
With Hermes, I just said, “Remember this meal data for next time under this name.” That was enough to make it part of the workflow. A feature I had postponed in the application became a one-request change.
For a personal workflow, I wasn't only skipping the initial development. I was also skipping much of the release cycle for every small improvement after it.

I Started Seeing the Same Question Elsewhere

I recently watched Theo Browne's talk Everything We Knew About Software Has Changed. His broader point was that capable models with tools such as code execution can reduce some ideas that once required custom software to a set of structured instructions. At one point, he joked that many startups in the room could be Markdown files.

That sounded less abstract after the NutriAgent experiment.

I also read @javz Start Building for Agents, Not Just Humans. In my comment, I connected his argument to personal agents that increasingly handle the interaction for us. If I already start from Hermes, a smaller application may not need to own the entire experience. It may only need to give my agent the missing behavior.

I don't think this means every AI product will become a Markdown file. NutriAgent is a personal workflow that writes to a destination I control. Products serving many people still have responsibilities around reliability, permissions, privacy, onboarding, support, and shared state. Some products are valuable precisely because they coordinate people or centralize something that an agent can't create on demand.

I don't have a clean answer for where that line is. But I know my first step has changed. A few months ago, I would've started a workflow like this by creating a repository. Today, I'd try it as a skill first and build the standalone product only when the workflow proves it needs one.

Now I Have to Ask This About Synapse

NutriAgent isn't the only AI product I've built.

Synapse, the personal AI companion I built for my wife, has a much more complex memory system. It ingests conversations, extracts entities and relationships, stores them in a temporal knowledge graph, and retrieves relevant context for future conversations. I've spent months improving its ingestion, GraphRAG retrieval, context budgets, and memory interfaces, It now serves more people, so multi-tenant is a thing.

Now I'm wondering how much of that system I would build if I started again today.

Could the ingestion workflow become a skill? Could a general-purpose agent use a graph tool and follow instructions for consolidating conversations? Is the graph architecture the product, or is it one implementation of the outcome I want?

I don't know.

Synapse may still need a dedicated product because reliable long-term memory, privacy, evaluation, and the experience around personal conversations could be the actual value. Or I may find that some of the system can be compressed just as NutriAgent was.

Most of my articles are about building more software to solve a problem. This one is about removing software while keeping the core workflow alive.

NutriAgent made sense as an application when I built it. What interests me now isn't whether that decision was correct. It's what kinds of products will still need to exist when personal agents can assemble workflows like this on demand.

Before I build my next AI product, I want to know how much of it can be a skill first.

If you are building AI products and want to follow along, I write about the problems and trade-offs that appear when someone actually uses them.

Follow me:

  • X: @juandastic
  • LinkedIn: Juan David Gomez

blocks.html_en:
The skill also changed how I add features. I had wanted predefined meals for food I had already analyzed on previous days. In NutriAgent, that would've meant changing the application, updating the prompt, testing the new behavior, and deploying another version.
With Hermes, I just said, “Remember this meal data for next time under this name.” That was enough to make it part of the workflow. A feature I had postponed in the application became a one-request change.
For a personal workflow, I wasn't only skipping the initial development. I was also skipping much of the release cycle for every small improvement after it.

sentences.en: The skill also changed how I add features.
sentences.zh: 这项技能还改变了我添加功能的方式。

sentences.en: I had wanted predefined meals for food I had already analyzed on previous days.
sentences.zh: 我想要预先准备好我前几天已经分析过的食物。

sentences.en: In NutriAgent, that would've meant changing the application, updating the prompt, testing the new behavior, and deploying another version.
sentences.zh: 在 NutriAgent 中，这意味着更改应用程序、更新提示、测试新行为以及部署另一个版本。

sentences.en: With Hermes, I just said, “Remember this meal data for next time under this name.”
sentences.zh: 对于 Hermes，我只是说：“记住这个用餐数据，下次用这个名字。”

sentences.en: That was enough to make it part of the workflow.
sentences.zh: 这足以使其成为工作流程的一部分。

sentences.en: A feature I had postponed in the application became a one-request change.
sentences.zh: 我在应用程序中推迟的一项功能变成了一次请求更改。

sentences.en: For a personal workflow, I wasn't only skipping the initial development.
sentences.zh: 对于个人工作流程，我不仅仅跳过了最初的开发。

sentences.en: I was also skipping much of the release cycle for every small improvement after it.
sentences.zh: 对于之后的每一个小改进，我也跳过了大部分发布周期。

blocks.html_en: I Started Seeing the Same Question Elsewhere

sentences.en: I Started Seeing the Same Question Elsewhere
sentences.zh: 我开始在其他地方看到同样的问题

blocks.html_en: I recently watched Theo Browne's talk Everything We Knew About Software Has Changed. His broader point was that capable models with tools such as code execution can reduce some ideas that once required custom software to a set of structured instructions. At one point, he joked that many startups in the room could be Markdown files.

sentences.en: I recently watched Theo Browne's talk Everything We Knew About Software Has Changed.
sentences.zh: 我最近观看了 Theo Browne 的演讲“我们所知道的关于软件的一切都已改变”。

sentences.en: His broader point was that capable models with tools such as code execution can reduce some ideas that once required custom software to a set of structured instructions.
sentences.zh: 他更广泛的观点是，具有代码执行等工具的功能模型可以将一些曾经需要定制软件的想法简化为一组结构化指令。

sentences.en: At one point, he joked that many startups in the room could be Markdown files.
sentences.zh: 有一次，他开玩笑说，房间里的许多初创公司可能都是 Markdown 文件。

blocks.html_en: That sounded less abstract after the NutriAgent experiment.

sentences.en: That sounded less abstract after the NutriAgent experiment.
sentences.zh: 经过 NutriAgent 实验之后，这听起来就不那么抽象了。

blocks.html_en: I also read @javz Start Building for Agents, Not Just Humans. In my comment, I connected his argument to personal agents that increasingly handle the interaction for us. If I already start from Hermes, a smaller application may not need to own the entire experience. It may only need to give my agent the missing behavior.

sentences.en: I also read @javz Start Building for Agents, Not Just Humans.
sentences.zh: 我还阅读了@javz Start Building for Agents，而不仅仅是人类。

sentences.en: In my comment, I connected his argument to personal agents that increasingly handle the interaction for us.
sentences.zh: 在我的评论中，我将他的论点与越来越多地为我们处理互动的个人代理人联系起来。

sentences.en: If I already start from Hermes, a smaller application may not need to own the entire experience.
sentences.zh: 如果我已经从 Hermes 开始，一个较小的应用程序可能不需要拥有整个体验。

sentences.en: It may only need to give my agent the missing behavior.
sentences.zh: 它可能只需要为我的代理提供缺失的行为即可。

blocks.html_en: I don't think this means every AI product will become a Markdown file. NutriAgent is a personal workflow that writes to a destination I control. Products serving many people still have responsibilities around reliability, permissions, privacy, onboarding, support, and shared state. Some products are valuable precisely because they coordinate people or centralize something that an agent can't create on demand.

sentences.en: I don't think this means every AI product will become a Markdown file.
sentences.zh: 我不认为这意味着每个 AI 产品都会变成 M​​arkdown 文件。

sentences.en: NutriAgent is a personal workflow that writes to a destination I control.
sentences.zh: NutriAgent 是一个个人工作流程，可写入我控制的目的地。

sentences.en: Products serving many people still have responsibilities around reliability, permissions, privacy, onboarding, support, and shared state.
sentences.zh: 为许多人服务的产品仍然在可靠性、权限、隐私、入门、支持和共享状态方面负有责任。

sentences.en: Some products are valuable precisely because they coordinate people or centralize something that an agent can't create on demand.
sentences.zh: 有些产品之所以有价值，正是因为它们可以协调人员或集中代理无法按需创建的东西。

blocks.html_en: I don't have a clean answer for where that line is. But I know my first step has changed. A few months ago, I would've started a workflow like this by creating a repository. Today, I'd try it as a skill first and build the standalone product only when the workflow proves it needs one.

sentences.en: I don't have a clean answer for where that line is.
sentences.zh: 对于这条线在哪里，我没有一个明确的答案。

sentences.en: But I know my first step has changed.
sentences.zh: 但我知道我的第一步已经改变了。

sentences.en: A few months ago, I would've started a workflow like this by creating a repository.
sentences.zh: 几个月前，我会通过创建存储库来启动这样的工作流程。

sentences.en: Today, I'd try it as a skill first and build the standalone product only when the workflow proves it needs one.
sentences.zh: 今天，我首先将其作为一项技能进行尝试，只有当工作流程证明需要一个独立产品时，我才会构建独立产品。

blocks.html_en: Now I Have to Ask This About Synapse

sentences.en: Now I Have to Ask This About Synapse
sentences.zh: 现在我必须问这个关于 Synapse 的问题

blocks.html_en: NutriAgent isn't the only AI product I've built.

sentences.en: NutriAgent isn't the only AI product I've built.
sentences.zh: NutriAgent 并不是我构建的唯一人工智能产品。

blocks.html_en: Synapse, the personal AI companion I built for my wife, has a much more complex memory system. It ingests conversations, extracts entities and relationships, stores them in a temporal knowledge graph, and retrieves relevant context for future conversations. I've spent months improving its ingestion, GraphRAG retrieval, context budgets, and memory interfaces, It now serves more people, so multi-tenant is a thing.

sentences.en: Synapse, the personal AI companion I built for my wife, has a much more complex memory system.
sentences.zh: Synapse 是我为妻子打造的个人人工智能伴侣，它的记忆系统要复杂得多。

sentences.en: It ingests conversations, extracts entities and relationships, stores them in a temporal knowledge graph, and retrieves relevant context for future conversations.
sentences.zh: 它摄取对话，提取实体和关系，将它们存储在时间知识图中，并检索未来对话的相关上下文。

sentences.en: I've spent months improving its ingestion, GraphRAG retrieval, context budgets, and memory interfaces, It now serves more people, so multi-tenant is a thing.
sentences.zh: 我花了几个月的时间改进它的摄取、GraphRAG 检索、上下文预算和内存接口，它现在为更多的人服务，所以多租户是一件事。

blocks.html_en: Now I'm wondering how much of that system I would build if I started again today.

sentences.en: Now I'm wondering how much of that system I would build if I started again today.
sentences.zh: 现在我想知道如果我今天重新开始，我会构建多少系统。

blocks.html_en: Could the ingestion workflow become a skill? Could a general-purpose agent use a graph tool and follow instructions for consolidating conversations? Is the graph architecture the product, or is it one implementation of the outcome I want?

sentences.en: Could the ingestion workflow become a skill?
sentences.zh: 摄取工作流程可以成为一项技能吗？

sentences.en: Could a general-purpose agent use a graph tool and follow instructions for consolidating conversations?
sentences.zh: 通用代理可以使用图形工具并遵循合并对话的说明吗？

sentences.en: Is the graph architecture the product, or is it one implementation of the outcome I want?
sentences.zh: 图形架构是产品，还是我想要的结果的一种实现？

blocks.html_en: I don't know.

sentences.en: I don't know.
sentences.zh: 我不知道。

blocks.html_en: Synapse may still need a dedicated product because reliable long-term memory, privacy, evaluation, and the experience around personal conversations could be the actual value. Or I may find that some of the system can be compressed just as NutriAgent was.

sentences.en: Synapse may still need a dedicated product because reliable long-term memory, privacy, evaluation, and the experience around personal conversations could be the actual value.
sentences.zh: Synapse 可能仍然需要专用产品，因为可靠的长期记忆、隐私、评估和个人对话体验可能才是真正的价值。

sentences.en: Or I may find that some of the system can be compressed just as NutriAgent was.
sentences.zh: 或者我可能会发现系统的某些部分可以像 NutriAgent 一样进行压缩。

blocks.html_en: Most of my articles are about building more software to solve a problem. This one is about removing software while keeping the core workflow alive.

sentences.en: Most of my articles are about building more software to solve a problem.
sentences.zh: 我的大多数文章都是关于构建更多软件来解决问题。

sentences.en: This one is about removing software while keeping the core workflow alive.
sentences.zh: 这是关于删除软件，同时保持核心工作流程的活力。

blocks.html_en: NutriAgent made sense as an application when I built it. What interests me now isn't whether that decision was correct. It's what kinds of products will still need to exist when personal agents can assemble workflows like this on demand.

sentences.en: NutriAgent made sense as an application when I built it.
sentences.zh: 当我构建 NutriAgent 作为一个应用程序时，它是有意义的。

sentences.en: What interests me now isn't whether that decision was correct.
sentences.zh: 现在我感兴趣的不是那个决定是否正确。

sentences.en: It's what kinds of products will still need to exist when personal agents can assemble workflows like this on demand.
sentences.zh: 当个人代理可以按需组装这样的工作流程时，仍然需要什么样的产品存在。

blocks.html_en: Before I build my next AI product, I want to know how much of it can be a skill first.

sentences.en: Before I build my next AI product, I want to know how much of it can be a skill first.
sentences.zh: 在构建下一个人工智能产品之前，我想首先知道它有多少可以成为一项技能。

blocks.html_en: If you are building AI products and want to follow along, I write about the problems and trade-offs that appear when someone actually uses them.

sentences.en: If you are building AI products and want to follow along, I write about the problems and trade-offs that appear when someone actually uses them.
sentences.zh: 如果您正在构建人工智能产品并想继续跟进，我会写一些人们实际使用它们时出现的问题和权衡。

blocks.html_en: Follow me:

sentences.en: Follow me:
sentences.zh: 跟我来：

blocks.html_en: • X: @juandastic

sentences.en: X: @juandastic
sentences.zh: X：@juandastic

blocks.html_en: • LinkedIn: Juan David Gomez

sentences.en: LinkedIn: Juan David Gomez
sentences.zh: LinkedIn：胡安·大卫·戈麦斯

content_html_zh:
当我在 2025 年 11 月第一次撰写有关 NutriAgent 的文章时，它是一个完整的应用程序。 它有一个 Python 后端、一个 Web 界面、一个 Telegram 机器人、用户帐户、Google OAuth、Supabase 和 Google Sheets 集成。

最近，我将其核心工作流程复制为我的个人人工智能代理的一项技能。 大约需要 15 分钟，并有两次提示。 我没有构建另一个后端、部署服务或再次实现 OAuth。 我解释了我希望工作流程如何运行，对其进行了测试，并观察了营养电子表格中出现的新行。

最初的申请并没有错误。 这就是我如何利用当时可用的工具来提供这种体验。

八个月后，起点发生了变化。

八个月前，这是一个应用程序

我构建 NutriAgent 是因为我想跟踪卡路里和蛋白质，而不会将数据困在营养应用程序中。 我想要我控制的电子表格中的原始记录，我可以在其中创建自己的报告并最终将营养与我的训练数据联系起来。

第一个版本是个人 n8n 工作流程。 它对我有用，但当一个朋友想尝试时，我意识到一切都与我的帐户相关联。 为了使其可重用，我用 Python 重新构建了它，并添加了真正的多用户产品所需的部分：身份验证、存储、Web 界面、Telegram、Google OAuth、对话历史记录和帐户链接。

我已经在《我放弃了 MyFitnessPal 并建立了一个人工智能代理来跟踪我的食物》中讲述了这个故事，后来又写了我每天使用它一个月后发生了什么问题。

本文从该版本之后开始。

我的游戏电脑变成了特工箱

我家里有一台普通的游戏电脑，配备 16 GB RAM 和 1 TB 驱动器。 通过 Windows、WSL 和 Mac 上的远程桌面使用它感觉很尴尬，所以我安装了 Linux 并将其变成一个用于运行代理的远程盒子。 我将单独写一下该设置。

我把 Hermes 从 VPS 移到了那里。 Hermes 是我现在在那台机器上运行的个人代理。 它可以加载可重复使用的技能并使用连接到我的 Google Workspace 的工具。 PC 运行代理环境，而不是前沿模型本身。

Hermes 运行后，我想测试一个我已经很了解的工作流程。 NutriAgent 是显而易见的候选者。

两次提示，十五分钟

我没有打开旧的存储库，而是描述了我想要的结果。 Hermes 应该了解一顿饭，估计其营养价值，遵循我现有日志的结构，并将结果附加到 Google 表格。

第一个提示创建了初始技能。 我使用第二个提示来澄清剩余的细节并调整它应该如何写入数据。

然后我尝试了一顿实际的饭菜。

结果与我最初构建 NutriAgent 来生成的电子表格相同。 它背后没有运行新的服务。 赫尔墨斯已经知道如何解释自然语言和使用工具。 Google Sheets 已经保存了数据。 该技能提供了我特有的部分：程序。

这就是令我惊讶的部分。 我没有找到更聪明的方法来重建 NutriAgent。 我根本没有重建它。

我不再需要构建什么

该技能并不等同于完整的 NutriAgent 产品。 我无法向某人提供注册链接。 它没有通用的 Telegram 机器人、自己的 Google OAuth 流程或专用界面。 它只需要与我的代理、我的 Google 帐户和我的电子表格配合使用。

对于个人使用，这足以保留我关心的行为：

  • 了解我吃了什么。
  • 估计卡路里和宏量。
  • 遵循我的营养日志的架构。
  • 将结果保存在我控制的地方。

以前，我必须同时拥有该行为及其周围的应用程序。 现在 Hermes 提供了大部分环境，而技能只是描述了这个工作流程的不同之处。

这也改变了边际成本。 我将 Hermes 从 VPS 中移除，并将此实验配置为使用我通过 Codex 订阅已经拥有的模型访问权限，而不是为 NutriAgent 维护单独的每次调用 API 账单。 订阅和电费不是免费的，因此这不是一个零成本的工作流程。 但它取消了每次 NutriAgent 交互的单独账单。

这项技能还改变了我添加功能的方式。 我想要预先准备好我前几天已经分析过的食物。 在 NutriAgent 中，这意味着更改应用程序、更新提示、测试新行为以及部署另一个版本。 对于 Hermes，我只是说：“记住这个用餐数据，下次用这个名字。” 这足以使其成为工作流程的一部分。 我在应用程序中推迟的一项功能变成了一次请求更改。 对于个人工作流程，我不仅仅跳过了最初的开发。 对于之后的每一个小改进，我也跳过了大部分发布周期。

我开始在其他地方看到同样的问题

我最近观看了 Theo Browne 的演讲“我们所知道的关于软件的一切都已改变”。 他更广泛的观点是，具有代码执行等工具的功能模型可以将一些曾经需要定制软件的想法简化为一组结构化指令。 有一次，他开玩笑说，房间里的许多初创公司可能都是 Markdown 文件。

经过 NutriAgent 实验之后，这听起来就不那么抽象了。

我还阅读了@javz Start Building for Agents，而不仅仅是人类。 在我的评论中，我将他的论点与越来越多地为我们处理互动的个人代理人联系起来。 如果我已经从 Hermes 开始，一个较小的应用程序可能不需要拥有整个体验。 它可能只需要为我的代理提供缺失的行为即可。

我不认为这意味着每个 AI 产品都会变成 M​​arkdown 文件。 NutriAgent 是一个个人工作流程，可写入我控制的目的地。 为许多人服务的产品仍然在可靠性、权限、隐私、入门、支持和共享状态方面负有责任。 有些产品之所以有价值，正是因为它们可以协调人员或集中代理无法按需创建的东西。

对于这条线在哪里，我没有一个明确的答案。 但我知道我的第一步已经改变了。 几个月前，我会通过创建存储库来启动这样的工作流程。 今天，我首先将其作为一项技能进行尝试，只有当工作流程证明需要一个独立产品时，我才会构建独立产品。

现在我必须问这个关于 Synapse 的问题

NutriAgent 并不是我构建的唯一人工智能产品。

Synapse 是我为妻子打造的个人人工智能伴侣，它的记忆系统要复杂得多。 它摄取对话，提取实体和关系，将它们存储在时间知识图中，并检索未来对话的相关上下文。 我花了几个月的时间改进它的摄取、GraphRAG 检索、上下文预算和内存接口，它现在为更多的人服务，所以多租户是一件事。

现在我想知道如果我今天重新开始，我会构建多少系统。

摄取工作流程可以成为一项技能吗？ 通用代理可以使用图形工具并遵循合并对话的说明吗？ 图形架构是产品，还是我想要的结果的一种实现？

我不知道。

Synapse 可能仍然需要专用产品，因为可靠的长期记忆、隐私、评估和个人对话体验可能才是真正的价值。 或者我可能会发现系统的某些部分可以像 NutriAgent 一样进行压缩。

我的大多数文章都是关于构建更多软件来解决问题。 这是关于删除软件，同时保持核心工作流程的活力。

当我构建 NutriAgent 作为一个应用程序时，它是有意义的。 现在我感兴趣的不是那个决定是否正确。 当个人代理可以按需组装这样的工作流程时，仍然需要什么样的产品存在。

在构建下一个人工智能产品之前，我想首先知道它有多少可以成为一项技能。

如果您正在构建人工智能产品并想继续跟进，我会写一些人们实际使用它们时出现的问题和权衡。

跟我来：

  • X：@juandastic
  • LinkedIn：胡安·大卫·戈麦斯
